onboarding.question:
1. Define what "ambient awareness only" means operationally — specifically, what happens if a logged-out user taps or interacts with the indicator itself, and whether it should ever become actionable or remain purely informational.
2. Specify the exact user experience when authentication state resolution succeeds between 2–60 seconds after initial load — does the user see a loading state, a skeleton screen, a blank canvas, or an immediate transition to authenticated home with conversation history appearing progressively.
3. Define the exact timing and content of the "clear, friendly message" surfaced when a user's session expires mid-sign-up — should it offer retry, suggest alternative recovery paths, or apologize and redirect to a fresh logged-out state.
4. Specify whether the in-conversation nudge (fired after first complete AI response) should appear inline in the message stream or as a separate UI overlay, and whether the user can dismiss it or if it persists until they act on the CTA.
5. Define the user experience when a logged-out user attempts to sign up but the account creation step fails — should they be offered retry, a contact support link, fallback to guest mode, or immediate sign-in attempt with a different provider.
6. Clarify what "the user transitions to authenticated state without visual disruption" means concretely — should the conversation history appear instantly, progressively load, or should there be a brief acknowledgment moment (e.g., "Welcome back" toast) before home screen renders.
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: The logged-out indicator is non-interactive text only — tapping it does nothing, it does not open any UI or menu, and it never becomes actionable; it remains purely informational at all times.

→ Rationale: Any interactivity converts ambient awareness into active UI, which violates the minimalist design principle and contradicts the "does not prompt sign-up" requirement; text-only ensures the indicator recedes behind the conversation.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[2]. My recommendation: When authentication state resolution succeeds between 2–60 seconds after initial load, the logged-out UI remains visible and interactive while the auth state updates silently in the background; upon successful resolution, conversation history loads progressively into the existing chat interface without spinners or skeleton screens, and the logged-out indicator disappears; the user sees no flash, flicker, or re-render.

→ Rationale: Progressive loading of history respects the "no visual disruption" requirement while giving the user immediate visibility that something has changed (indicator disappearance); loading history into the existing interface preserves conversation continuity.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[3]. My recommendation: When a user's session expires mid-sign-up (before authentication completes), an inline message appears at the top of the chat interface stating "Your session expired before sign-up completed. Your conversation couldn't be saved. You can start a new conversation and try signing up again" with a single "Start fresh" button that clears the chat and returns to logged-out mode; no retry of sign-up is offered, as the conversation is already lost.

→ Rationale: This acknowledges the failure clearly and honestly without false hope of recovery (the conversation is gone); offering a fresh start respects user agency while moving forward; no retry loop prevents frustration.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[4]. My recommendation: The in-conversation nudge appears inline in the message stream as a conversational system message rendered directly after the first complete AI response; it is dismissible (a close affordance is visible), and dismissing it does not interrupt the conversation or prevent further exchanges.

→ Rationale: Inline system messages preserve the conversational, minimalist aesthetic; dismissibility respects user agency without forcing sign-up; inlining keeps chrome minimal and lets the conversation lead.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[5]. My recommendation: When account creation fails post-SSO, the user is returned to the logged-out chat interface (preserving conversation context) with an inline error message in the Auth Sheet that reads "Account creation failed. Please try again" with a single retry button; if retry fails a second time, the same button remains enabled with additional text ("Check your connection or contact support@health360.com").

→ Rationale: Returning to the chat preserves context and respects the principle that the logged-out experience is never blocked; inline retry in the Auth Sheet avoids navigation friction; second failure provides diagnostic help without being punitive.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

[6]. My recommendation: "Without visual disruption" means the transition from logged-out to authenticated state occurs with zero loading spinners, skeleton screens, full-page reloads, or visible re-renders — the logged-out UI remains visible, the logged-out indicator disappears, conversation history loads silently and progressively into the existing chat interface, and no acknowledgment moment (toast, banner, or "Welcome back" message) appears.

→ Rationale: Eliminating all visual feedback keeps the transition truly seamless and respects the minimalist design; the disappearance of the indicator itself signals authentication has succeeded; silent history loading preserves focus on the conversation.

→ Note: Pending human PM confirmation before engineering handoff

---

Once you approve these recommendations, I'll update the product spec to reflect each confirmed decision.
